vulnerabilities.title: DEBUG mode enabled in production
vulnerabilities.description: Application is running with DEBUG=True in production environment, exposing sensitive configuration details via error pages.
vulnerabilities.impact: Full environment variable disclosure, potential RCE via debug console
vulnerabilities.fix_description: Set DEBUG=False in production settings and verify with environment variable override

vulnerabilities.title: CORS allows all origins
vulnerabilities.description: CORS policy permits requests from any origin, enabling cross-origin data theft.
vulnerabilities.impact: Data exfiltration, CSRF-style attacks from any domain
vulnerabilities.fix_description: Restrict Access-Control-Allow-Origin to specific trusted domains

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded AWS secret key in source code
vulnerabilities.description: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY is hardcoded in source file config.py.
vulnerabilities.impact: Full AWS account compromise if repository is exposed
vulnerabilities.fix_description: Move to environment variables and rotate the compromised key

vulnerabilities.title: Missing HSTS header
vulnerabilities.description: HTTP Strict-Transport-Security header is not set on HTTPS responses.

vulnerabilities.title: Outdated jQuery version (3.4.1)
vulnerabilities.description: jQuery 3.4.1 has known XSS vulnerabilities. Latest version is 3.7.1.
vulnerabilities.impact: Potential XSS via jQuery methods if user input reaches them

recommendations: Rotate the exposed AWS key immediately
Enable HSTS for all HTTPS responses
Update jQuery and other outdated dependencies
